title: Priority field: first-class P0-P4 on the issue record (v2.8 spec bump)
body:
# Goal

First-class `priority` field on the issue record. Today
jjforge has no priority concept; teams encode it via labels
(`p0` / `p1` / ad-hoc). That works but is unscanned,
unsorted, and not surfaced anywhere — `jjf ready`'s sort
order doesn't know about it; `jjf ls` doesn't show it; it's
just a label like any other.

Beads makes priority a first-class P0..P4 field (lower =
higher priority) and uses it as the primary `ready` sort
key. The asterinas tree already has thousands of issues that
would benefit from the same dimension. This ticket adds it
to jjforge with a v2.7 → v2.8 spec bump.

# Why now

- The `ls-row-rework` ticket (`b74b156`) is the natural
  vehicle to add a column. Doing it before that ships
  lets us add `priority` and `type` columns in one row
  rework rather than two.
- `host-asterinas-migrate` (`cc2fa96`) will benefit from
  the field being first-class before the bulk import — the
  audit's mapping rules can target a structured `priority`
  field instead of munging labels.
- The agent-orchestration loop ranks `jjf ready` output;
  priority is the missing dimension.

# Spec bump shape

v2.7 → v2.8 (backwards compat per the v2.1 template at
`docs/storage-format.md:299-327`):

- **New record field `priority`** (integer 0–4, default
  `null` meaning unspecified). Placed between `type` and
  `labels` in the on-disk record.
  - Value space: `0..=4`. `null` is the default for any
    record whose creator didn't choose. Use `null` (not a
    sentinel `5` or `-1`) so the serde shape is honest
    about "unset" vs "set to lowest."
  - Lower number = higher priority (P0 = ship-stopping,
    P4 = whenever). Mirrors beads.
- **New op `set-priority`** — payload field `Jjf-Priority`.
  Integer string in `0..=4`, or empty value to clear back
  to `null`.
- **Create-time emission (§5.7)** — non-null `priority`
  values emit a `set-priority` stanza in the multi-op create
  commit. Emission order matches record-field order:
  slug, body, type, priority, labels, dependencies,
  assignee.
- Backwards compat:
  - v2.7 readers tolerate v2.8 records — extra field, serde
    ignores it (same path the v2.1 type/slug additions
    took).
  - v2.7 readers seeing a `set-priority` op trailer fall
    through the unknown-op tolerance in §5.2 — no error,
    just no effect (audit log preserved).
  - v2.8 readers seeing v2.7 records get `priority = null`
    via serde-default.

# CLI surface

- `jjf new ... -p <0-4>` or `--priority <0-4>`. Optional.
  Default omits the flag → record gets `null`.
- `jjf update <id> --priority <0-4>` and
  `jjf update <id> --unset-priority` (mirrors the
  `--assignee` / `--unset-assignee` three-way pattern).
- `jjf ls --priority <0-4>` filter. Repeatable (OR semantics
  on the list; an issue matches if its priority is any of
  the requested values).
- `jjf ready --priority <0-4>` — same filter.
- `jjf ls` plain-text row shows priority as `P0`..`P4` or
  blank for `null`. Coordinate with `ls-row-rework`
  (`b74b156`) — the two should land together as one
  combined row shape: `<id>\t<status>\t<priority>\t<type>\t<title>`
  OR `<id>\t<status>\t<type>\t<priority>\t<title>` — pick
  one when implementing.
- `jjf show <id>` plain-text adds a `priority:` line between
  the existing `assignee:` and `dependencies:` lines.
- `jjf show <id> --json` exposes `"priority": null` or
  `"priority": 2`.

# Sort behavior

- `jjf ready` v2.8: primary sort key becomes `priority`
  (nulls last — unspecified issues sort after explicit
  P4). Secondary keys unchanged (type priority,
  created_at). Document the new sort order in
  `docs/cli-json.md`'s `ready` section.
- `jjf ls` sort unchanged (created_at descending). Don't
  silently change `ls`'s sort — that's a separate
  decision.

# Validation

- Parser rejects integer values outside `0..=4` with a
  typed error (kind: `invalid_priority`, exit 2, preflight).
- CLI parser rejects non-integer strings the same way.
- Empty payload on the `set-priority` op trailer clears
  the field to `null` (mirrors `set-slug` clearing).

# Out of scope

- Renaming or repurposing the existing `p0`/`p1`/etc.
  labels in this repo's planner data. A separate hygiene
  pass can convert them after this ships.
- Priority on memories. Memories don't have a priority.
- More than five buckets. Beads' P0..P4 is good enough.
- Auto-sorting `jjf ls` by priority. Opt-in via a future
  `--sort` flag if we want it; not silently.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `priority` field landed in
      `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs`, default `null`,
      between `type` and `labels`.
- [ ] `set-priority` op and `Jjf-Priority` trailer
      implemented per §5.5 / §5.7.
- [ ] `Storage::set_priority(id, Option<u8>)` mutator
      (and `Storage::update`'s `UpdateFields::priority`
      sibling, mirroring the `assignee` three-way
      `Option<Option<...>>` shape).
- [ ] CLI flags landed for `new`, `update`,
      `--unset-priority`, `ls --priority`, `ready --priority`.
- [ ] `ready` sort uses `priority` as primary key with
      nulls last; updated test asserts the order.
- [ ] `docs/storage-format.md` v2.8 changelog section.
- [ ] `docs/cli-json.md` updated.
- [ ] Tests: round-trip new field, set / clear via update,
      invalid-priority rejection, ready ordering, ls
      filter, show rendering.

# Depends on

This ticket sequences after `b74b156 ls-row-rework`
ideally, OR the two land in one combined commit (since
both touch the same row format and that minimizes test
churn). Implementer's call.

# Cross-refs

- Inspiration: `reference/beads/cmd/bd/list_format.go:42-72`
  (the default-mode formatter shows `STATUS_ICON ID PRIORITY
  [Type] Title`).
- Spec-bump template: v2 → v2.1 `type` field addition at
  `docs/storage-format.md:299-327`.
